dimensions.note: No secrets handled or stored. Uses the ambient AWS credential chain (ambient target) or per-profile fromIni({ profile }); region/profiles/suffix are non-secret globalArguments. Nothing is vaulted and no credential material is logged — warn logs carry only the profile label and the classified error message.

info on per-account scan start (account/profile/region count) and a final completion summary (finding/error counts); warn on suffix-skip, credential failure, per-region scan failure, and a GroupId-less default SG. All structured placeholders ({profile}, {region}, {message}, …); no secrets in messages or properties.

Failure isolation is deliberate and correct: getAccountId, describeEnabledRegions, and the per-region SG/VPC/ENI block are each wrapped so a failure becomes a writeError(scan_error) row (classified via classifyError) and the loop continues. writeError increments errorCount before writing, so the returned counts match the writes. The suffix check fails closed before any AWS call. No throw can occur after a partial finding is written for the same SG (writeResource is the last statement per SG).

tests/ holds a unit suite (pure helpers + runScan via createModelTestContext with an injected AwsApi replay) and a smoke suite (fan-out across accounts/regions, expired-account isolation, handle-count invariant, and the real model.methods.scan.execute entrypoint degrading to a scan_error without throwing). The classifyError block is the regression bed — it locks in that an SSO role ARN inside an unrelated AccessDenied message stays access_denied. The colocated aws_default_sg_audit_test.ts pins model/manifest name+version sync, the resource/method key sets, and the upgrade-chain-ends-at-version invariant. Both success and failure paths covered.

Read-only sweep with deterministic, content-stable instance keys: findingKey(accountId, region, sgId) and scanErrorKey(profile, region, phase). Re-runs overwrite the same instances rather than duplicating (a test asserts identical names across two contexts). No created cloud resources to orphan; a partial sweep simply leaves fewer rows, each individually consistent.

DescribeSecurityGroups, DescribeVpcs, and DescribeNetworkInterfaces all paginate to exhaustion via NextToken; SGs are server-side filtered to group-name=default and ENIs to group-id. All AWS SDK shapes are treated as optional and defaulted before use (?? '', ?? [], ?? false). Throttling is handled by the SDK retryer (maxAttempts: 5). describeVpcs short-circuits on an empty id list to avoid an invalid request.

EC2Client is created per region and STSClient per account inside the facade; both are AWS SDK clients used only for read calls and need no explicit teardown. No file handles, sockets, or temp files are opened, and no cloud resources are created, so there is nothing to track for cleanup on any code path.

The prior review recorded an ISSUE: tests/aws_default_sg_audit_test.ts:99 (the classifyError regression fixture) embedded a real 12-digit account id (688375758630), an internal SSO permission-set name (AWSReservedSSO_SitooReadOnlyAccess_xyz), and an abbreviated real-looking email (m@s.com). It is now scrubbed to a fully synthetic ARN (arn:aws:sts::111111111111:assumed-role/AWSReservedSSO_ReadOnlyAccess_abc123/alice@example.com) that preserves the AWSReservedSSO_ substring and the AccessDenied 'not authorized' wording, so the regression keeps its teeth (still asserts access_denied). grep -rniE 'sitoo|vanta|aikido|688375758630|m@s\.com' over the whole extension dir now returns zero hits; the README and model entrypoint were already clean (no IPs, hostnames, or real account ids). Zero real infrastructure or vendor identifiers remain on the published surface.

FindingSchema, ScanErrorSchema, EniSchema, and GlobalArgsSchema are all plain z.object with no .passthrough(); tags use z.record(z.string(), z.string()), verdict/kind are z.enum. Public resource shapes (Finding, Eni, ScanError, Verdict) are declared as explicit interfaces/union types rather than z.infer, keeping deno doc --lint clean.
Both specs (finding, scan_error) declare lifetime: 'infinite' and garbageCollection: 10.

Read-only audit/discovery model, not a CRUD lifecycle. The single scan method is a factory that re-derives every finding + scan_error each run; there is no AWS resource this model owns to create/update/delete, and re-running scan is itself the drift-detection mechanism, so a separate sync method is unnecessary.

scan performs only read-only Describe*/sts:GetCallerIdentity calls; there is no mutating action a policy/live preflight label would gate. (The requiredProfileSuffix fail-closed guard is a config gate, not a mutation preflight.)

Mechanical check: finding keys are 'finding-<account>-<region>-<sgId>' and scan_error keys are 'error-<profile|ambient>-<region|account>-<phase>', so the two specs share no key prefix and cannot collide. Each spec is written by a single code path (writeResource for finding inside the SG loop; writeError for scan_error), each conforming to its own schema. Schema-write conformance verified: the finding object carries all 15 FindingSchema fields and each Eni all 7 EniSchema fields; the ScanError object carries all 7 ScanErrorSchema fields — 1:1 coverage, no placeholder-stuffed fields.

Single-pass factory writer; runScan only calls context.writeResource and never reads back previously stored swamp state (no update/delete/sync method exists), so the readResource-vs-dataRepository concern does not arise.

Initial publish. model.upgrades carries a single no-op entry whose toVersion (2026.06.13.0) equals model.version, establishing the chain so any future schema-changing upgrade chains cleanly from here; the colocated test asserts this invariant. globalArguments are unchanged, so the identity upgradeAttributes (old) => old is correct.
